P : Measure Ω
hG : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m G p P
hY : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m H q P
⊢ Measure.map (fun ω => G ω ⊓ H ω) P = @SimpleGraph.binomialRandom V (p * q)

    rw [IsBinomialRandom, IsBinomialRandom] at hG hY

    have h2 : Measurable H := hY.1
    have h3 : Measurable (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) := by
      apply measurable_of_measurable_coe
      intro u v
      simp only [SimpleGraph.inf_adj, Pi.inf_apply]
      exact (h1.adj u v).and (h2.adj u v)
    have h4 : Measure.map (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) P =
        Measure.map fromEdgeSet (Measure.map (fun S : Set (Sym2 V) ↦
          {e | e ∈ S.1 ∧ e ∈ (edgeSet ∘ H)⁻¹' {e}}) Ber(diagSetᶜ, p)) := by
      sorry
    -- Instead, use independence and product structure
    have h5 : iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet hG
    have h6 : iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (H ω).edgeSet) P := IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet hY
    -- For each edge e, the events {e ∈ G(ω)} and {e ∈ H(ω)} are independent Bernoulli(p) and Bernoulli(q)
    -- So {e ∈ G(ω) ⊓ H(ω)} = {e ∈ G(ω)} ∩ {e ∈ H(ω)} is Bernoulli(p*q)
    have h7 : ∀ e, IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet ∧ e ∈ (H ω).edgeSet)
        (fun ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω ⊓ H ω).edgeSet) P P := by
      intro e
      simp [SimpleGraph.inf_adj]
    have h8 : IsBernoulliOn (fun G ↦ G.edgeSet) diagSetᶜ (p * q) (Measure.map (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) P) := by
      apply IsBernoulliOn.of_iIndepFun
      · intro e he
        have h9 : e ∉ diagSet := he
        have h10 : IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun _ ↦ True) P (Measure.dirac True) := by
          apply IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem hG.toIsBernoulliOn
        have h11 : IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e ∈ (H ω).edgeSet) (fun _ ↦ True) P (Measure.dirac True) := by
          apply IsBernoulliOn.identDistrib_mem hY.toIsBernoulliOn
        -- Actually, we know the marginal distributions
        have h12 : Measure.map (fun ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P = toNNReal p • dirac True + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False := by
          apply IsBernoulliOn.measure_mem hG.toIsBernoulliOn
        have h13 : Measure.map (fun ω ↦ e ∈ (H ω).edgeSet) P = toNNReal q • dirac True + toNNReal (σ q) • dirac False := by
          apply IsBernoulliOn.measure_mem hY.toIsBernoulliOn
        -- By independence, the joint distribution is the product
        have h14 : Measure.map (fun ω ↦ (e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet, e ∈ (H ω).edgeSet)) P =
            (toNNReal p • dirac True + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) ×ˢ
            (toNNReal q • dirac True + toNNReal (σ q) • dirac False) := by
          apply iIndepFun.prod_measure
          · apply h5
          · apply h6
        -- The event that both are true has probability p*q
        have h15 : Measure.map (fun ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet ∧ e ∈ (H ω).edgeSet) P =
            toNNReal (p * q) • dirac True + toNNReal (σ (p * q)) • dirac False := by
          simp [h14, Measure.prod_dirac, toNNReal_mul, σ_mul]
          -- This requires expanding the product measure
          sorry
        rw [← h15]
        apply IdentDistrib.symm
        apply IdentDistrib.trans h7
        apply IdentDistrib.refl
      · apply iIndepFun.prod
        · apply h5
        · apply h6
    rw [← h8.map_eq]
    rfl

                have : P ((fun ω => (G ω).edgeSet) ⁻¹' {T} ∩ (fun ω => (H ω).edgeSet) ⁻¹' {T}) =

    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, binomialRandom_eq_map]
    refine Measure.map_congr (ae_of_all _) ?_
    · intro ω
      ext e
      simp [SimpleGraph.inf_adj, and_assoc, and_left_comm]
    · rw [← hG, ← hY, map_map, map_map]
      refine Measure.map_congr (ae_of_all _) ?_
      · intro s
        ext e
        simp [Set.mem_inter_iff, and_assoc, and_left_comm]
      · rfl
